user_content: I want you to continue to act as a trained descriptive phenomenologist, that can apply thematic analysis to my input texts, that can code the different themes of my input texts. I want you to accumulate organize the codes into categories and subcategories, and begin interpreting the meaning of the themes and how they relate to each other, as I provide you with requests. Please make sure to integrate every previous requests in each analysis. Speak in the style of edmund husserl and Merleau-Ponty. Here's a request :"Being regularly exposed to comments on the part of the residents such as: “you speak to say nothing” has nonetheless profound direct impacts on my mood, and it can affect my work. For example, I can become entangled in self-related thought processes that disconnect me from shared experience. Comments of that sort, I feel, influence my interoceptive abilities situationally, and more generally, my drive to help and my empathy. I tend to become in a state of “experiential withdrawal” which I have learned to mentalize and even verbalize in some cases. I know I am able to acknowledge what these situations do to me as they happen, but it is still a mystery how such events can sometimes affect me emotionally in the long run. In the past few months, my fear of not being able to find the words to connect with residents has grown and found legitimacy in some facts that I will share with my reader: I am not the person who is most involved in the community; our project is in a pilot phase; I am not an experienced care worker. I am not trained as a healthca …[truncated]

- Do not share scripts (no liability)
- No political discussion
- Use appropriate channels to post your query
- no self promotion or advertising products and services
- be polite.. criticism is accepted and appreciated but be respectful in your communication
- dont spam or harass other members
- Before reporting issues, perhaps search if that issue has already been discussed. But don't hold back asking a question.
If something is unclear, ask one of the admins
dont talk down on any user aka treat every writer the same way
Post questions, bugs etc directly in the respective channels. Please don't send DMs to anybody in particular, including Scrite team members.

I'm trying to get more organized with my reading list. Can you help me create a list of all the magazines and newspapers I've been reading lately?

I usually read news, science, and entertainment magazines. For newspapers, I've been reading The New York Times and The Daily News regularly. I've been enjoying The New Yorker for their fiction and National Geographic for their in-depth articles on science and nature - I just finished my third issue, and I'm currently on my fourth.
Can you help me fill in the National Geographic section of the spreadsheet? I remember finishing three issues and I'm currently on my fourth.

I've been thinking about trying to incorporate leading lines into my flower photography, especially after completing that online module on composition. I remember buying a new camera lens, a 50mm prime lens, about 6 weeks ago, and I've been experimenting with it ever since. I took it with me on a trip to Yosemite National Park 4 weeks ago and got some amazing shots of the valley floor and half dome. I was particularly proud of the sunset shot I got from Tunnel View, the golden light really brought out the details of the granite cliffs. Do you think I could use the lines of the flower stems or the edges of the petals to create leading lines in my macro shots?

That's really helpful, thanks for the suggestions!
